title: Redux Fundamentals

learningObjectives.text: Explain store using a new example and justify the result.
Explain reducers using a new example and justify the result.
Explain actions using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain unidirectional state flow using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use redux fundamentals without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **Redux** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Store** → **Reducers** → **Actions** → **Unidirectional state flow**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Store
sections.body: A Redux store holds the application state tree and coordinates dispatch, reducers, subscriptions, and middleware.

sections.title: Reducers
sections.body: Redux reducers calculate next state from current state and an action. They must be predictable and side-effect free.

sections.title: Actions
sections.body: Redux actions are plain event descriptions with a type and optional payload.

sections.title: Unidirectional state flow
sections.body: UI dispatches an action, reducers calculate state, and subscribed UI reads the new state.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: An action describes an event and the reducer calculates the next state.

commonMistakes: Putting every local input into Redux.
Performing side effects inside a reducer.
Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
summary: Redux Fundamentals connects Store, Reducers, Actions.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Which change is the best fix?

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
